mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate increases discount rates and signals tightening liquidity, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and declining unemployment rate confirm labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 when PAYEMS rises but UNRATE stagnates, indicating the joint signal is not predictive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate durable demand expansion, which lifts forward equity returns specifically during early-cycle recoveries when capacity utilization is low.
failure_condition: Signal turns negative during late-cycle expansions when housing demand decouples from broad economic growth.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment dropping below 6% signals peak labor market tightness, forcing wage growth and supporting consumer demand, which lifts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX falls >2% when UNRATE crosses below 6%, contradicting the predicted positive return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable consumer prices indicates favorable supply-demand balance, expanding profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative when INDPRO rises but CPIAUCSL spikes >2% annually, showing inflation erodes margin expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities, mechanically depressing forward returns over short horizons regardless of economic cycle phase.
failure_condition: Positive correlation persists over 5-year rolling windows, indicating the rate-return relationship has structurally broken down.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with expanding real GDP confirm broad-based demand growth, which strongly lifts equity returns specifically during post-recession recoveries when earnings revisions are positive.
failure_condition: Signal fails during stagflationary periods where GDP stagnates but retail sales rise, producing negative forward returns.
